mechanism_narrative: Industrial production trends reflect real economic activity, and persistent increases in INDPRO signal expanding output and corporate earnings, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO growth and forward 60-day returns is less than 0.05 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6%, it signals severe labor market slack that prompts expansionary fiscal and monetary policy responses, which in turn boost equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6%, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is negative more than 50% of the time, contradicting the predicted positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate broad-based consumer and housing demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST increase, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime where the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, further monetary easing is ineffective and the low rate signals economic distress, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound and economic weakness
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, the median 20-day S&P 500 return is positive, contradicting the predicted negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth reflects the overall health of the economy; positive GDP growth leads to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is less than 0.1 over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.3% raises expectations of Fed tightening, which negatively impacts equity prices within a short 5-day window.
failure_condition: When CPI month-over-month change > 0.3%, the average 5-day SPX return is positive, contradicting negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment increases (positive PAYEMS change) and the fed funds rate is below 2%, the labor market is strengthening with accommodative monetary policy, a combination that is strongly positive for equities over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The composite signal has a hit ratio below 55% or average return not significantly positive over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales exceed $400 billion, it signals potential consumer overheating that may lead to spending pullback or Fed tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When RSAFS > 400000, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 60% of observations, contradicting negative prediction.
